rag_rules: Insert AGREE annex blocks into implementation scope when bindings depend on subcomponents or connections, and into type scope when referring only to visible component features.
generic_rules: Preserve AADL scope boundaries: component type annexes may only reference visible ports, not subcomponent paths.
Implementation annexes may bind outputs or internal states but must not reference non-visible or non-declared elements.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: Use only AGREE scalar types: int, real, bool; avoid integer literals for Boolean comparisons.
Guarantees and assumptions must use only features declared at the scope level of the annex.
anti_patterns: Do not reference subcomponent ports or internal paths in component type annexes.
Do not encode event ports as numeric or Boolean values unless explicitly mapped to data ports in the AADL model.
